entry.title: Fly Client Proposal
entry.summary:
FlyClient presents a notable advantage over SPV (Simplified Payment Verification) clients for blockchains that generate a high volume of headers daily, significantly more than those seen in Bitcoin's architecture. This distinction underlines the enhanced efficiency and capability that FlyClient can offer in handling larger scales of blockchain data.
However, the necessity for implementing FlyClient as a soft fork within the Bitcoin network may not be as critical as previously thought. A viable alternative exists in the form of STARK proofs, which can effectively substitute the need for such a soft fork. The application of STARK proofs is well-documented and can be explored further through the resources provided on [GitHub](https://github.com/starkware-bitcoin/raito). Given this alternative, efforts and resources might be more judiciously allocated towards other areas of development and consensus building within the Bitcoin community, especially considering the impending phase where Bitcoin becomes fully ossified, making any protocol changes increasingly difficult to enact.
